mission.title: Install and Configure ExternalDNS
mission.description: ExternalDNS is an operator that synchronizes Kubernetes Services and Ingresses with DNS providers, allowing for dynamic DNS record management. It is particularly useful in environments where services are frequently created and destroyed, ensuring that DNS records are always up-to-date. Key trade-offs include dependency on external DNS providers and potential delays in DNS propagation.

steps.title: Add the ExternalDNS Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official ExternalDNS Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add external-dns https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami

helm repo update

```

> This repository contains the ExternalDNS Helm chart.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install ExternalDNS with Helm
steps.description:
This step installs ExternalDNS in the specified namespace with the necessary configurations.

```bash

helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --create-namespace --version 0.20.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

```

> Replace 'aws' with your DNS provider as needed.
commands: helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --create-namespace --version 0.20.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

steps.title: Verify ExternalDNS is running
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the ExternalDNS deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-dns

kubectl logs -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-dns

```

> You should see the ExternalDNS pod running.
> Check the logs for any errors or confirmation of DNS updates.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ExternalDNS
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ExternalDNS deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to orphaned DNS records in your DNS provider.

```bash

helm uninstall external-dns -n kube-system

```

> This command removes the ExternalDNS release.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ExternalDNS
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current configuration. This step upgrades ExternalDNS to the latest version.

```bash

helm get values external-dns -n kube-system > external-dns-backup.yaml

helm upgrade external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.20.0

kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-dns

```

> Backup current values for ExternalDNS.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the new version is running.
commands: helm get values external-dns -n kube-system > external-dns-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No valid zones found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that ExternalDNS cannot find any zones configured for the specified provider.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the correct credentials and zone configurations are set in the ExternalDNS deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to update DNS records'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions for the DNS provider.

**Fix:**
Verify that the IAM roles or API keys used by ExternalDNS have the necessary permissions to modify DNS records.

troubleshooting.title: DNS records not updating
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to caching issues or propagation delays with the DNS provider.

**Fix:**
Check the DNS provider's dashboard for the current state of records and ensure ExternalDNS is running without errors.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the ExternalDNS pod is failing to start, possibly due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs for detailed error messages and ensure all required environment variables are set correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Azure DNS and improved logging for DNS updates.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the ExternalDNS pod running with a status of 'Running' and the expected DNS records created in your DNS provider's dashboard.
codeSnippets: helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --create-namespace --version 0.20.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
